mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter as corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no significant difference in forward returns compared to baseline or IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during periods of high market volatility.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when VIX is below 15, or positive returns occur in >70% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 2.5% force expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation months or correlation with forward returns drops below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year IC falls below 0.02 or positive returns occur in less than 55% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with subdued inflation creates a soft-landing environment that boosts equity risk premiums, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive edge over single-series models or forward returns are negative in >50% of aligned periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden acceleration in industrial production above 1.0% month-over-month signals robust manufacturing demand and supply chain utilization, triggering short-term positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal generates negative returns in >60% of triggered instances or loses significance when oil prices spike above $65 per barrel.
